name: Mane Anchor
region: Oceania, Melanesia, Solomon Islands, Central Province, Mane
description: Tucked in the Solomon Sea, this site features a massive anchor resting on a vibrant reef slope. Schools of barracuda and trevally patrol the blue, while colorful soft corals and sponges cover the structure. The anchor itself is encrusted with marine life, attracting curious reef fish. Visibility is typically excellent, with gentle currents ideal for drift diving. The surrounding reef teems with critters like nudibranchs and shrimp, making it a macro lover’s paradise. Depths range from 10 to 30 meters, suitable for all experience levels. Warm waters and minimal surge create comfortable diving year-round.

name: 3Rd Mooring
region: Oceania, Melanesia, Solomon Islands, Western Province, Mbulo Island
description: This dive site near Mbulo Island offers vibrant coral gardens teeming with reef fish, barracuda, and occasional reef sharks. The sloping reef transitions to a sandy bottom at 30 meters, with excellent visibility often exceeding 20 meters. Gentle currents attract schooling fish, while overhangs shelter colorful critters. Warm waters and minimal surge make it ideal for all skill levels. The site's healthy hard corals and frequent turtle sightings highlight its pristine condition.

name: Wreck Of The Ann
region: Oceania, Melanesia, Solomon Islands, Central Province, Mbanika
description: This WWII Japanese transport ship lies upright at 30 meters near Mbanika. The wreck is encrusted with colorful corals and sponges, attracting schools of batfish, barracuda, and reef sharks. Excellent visibility and mild currents make it accessible for intermediate divers. The intact hull offers swim-throughs, while the deck reveals remnants of its wartime past. Nearby reefs teem with tropical fish, adding to the dive's variety. Best visited between April and November for calm conditions.

name: Nembalau Point
region: Oceania, Melanesia, Solomon Islands, Central Province, Mbokonimbeti
description: Nembalau Point offers vibrant coral walls teeming marine life in the Solomon Sea. Strong currents attract pelagics like barracuda and reef sharks. The steep drop-off reveals colorful soft corals and schools of fusiliers. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters. Best dived during slack tide when conditions calm. Nearby Mbokonimbeti village provides easy access to this pristine site.

name: Leru Cut
region: Oceania, Melanesia, Solomon Islands, Central Province, Pavuvu
description: This dive site off Pavuvu features a dramatic underwater canyon with steep walls covered in vibrant soft corals. Strong currents attract pelagic species like barracuda and reef sharks, while the canyon's crevices shelter smaller critters. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters in these nutrient-rich waters. The site's unique topography creates thrilling drift dives along the canyon's edge, with occasional sightings of passing eagle rays. Best suited for experienced divers due to the currents.

The healthy hard corals and occasional WWII artifacts add historical intrigue. Best dived during calm seasons when the Solomon Sea conditions are optimal.

name: Japanese Seaplane
region: Oceania, Melanesia, Solomon Islands, Central Province, Tanambogho
description: This WWII wreck rests in the Solomon Sea near Tanambogho, offering a haunting yet fascinating dive. The intact Japanese seaplane lies at 18 meters, covered in soft corals and surrounded by schools of tropical fish. Excellent visibility and calm currents make it accessible for most divers. Explore the cockpit and wings, now home to crustaceans and nudibranchs. Nearby reefs teem with colorful marine life, adding to the site's appeal. A must for history buffs and underwater photographers.
